content: read reviews/530.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/530.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/530.md.

1→# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/530.lean`
     2→
     3→Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
     4→without an explicit justification.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## 1. Copyright Header
     9→
    10→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
    11→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
    12→
    13→## 2. Import Statement
    14→
    15→- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
    16→- [x] No other imports are present.
    17→
    18→## 3. Module Docstring
    19→
    20→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    21→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 530`.
    22→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/530`.
    23→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines ℓ(N) as the maximum Sidon subset size guaranteed in any N-element real set, asks whether ℓ(N) ~ N^{1/2}).
    24→- [x] Bibliography entries present: [Er73], [Er75f], [Er80e], [Ri69], [KSS75].
    25→
    26→## 4. Namespace
    27→
    28→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos530 ... end Erdos530`.
    29→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos530`).
    30→- [x] `open Finset Filter` appears **before** the namespace block (line 41).
    31→
    32→## 5. Custom Definitions
    33→
    34→- [x] `IsSidonSet` (line 48) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 45–47).
    35→- [x] `maxSidonSubsetSize` (line 53) has a LaTeX docstring (line 52).
    36→- [x] `sidonSubsetNumber` (line 59) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 56–58).
    37→- [x] `maxSidonSubsetSize` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sSup`, which involves classical choice).
    38→- [x] `sidonSubsetNumber` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`, which involves classical choice).
    39→- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
    40→- [x] Prop uses `UpperCamelCase`: `IsSidonSet`.
    41→- [x] Functions returning values use `lowerCamelCase`: `maxSidonSubsetSize`, `sidonSubsetNumber`.
    42→- N/A: No `local notation` used (none needed).
    43→- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional; "consider" item).
    44→
    45→## 6. Theorem Statements
    46→
    47→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    48→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_530` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
    49→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]`.
    50→- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 62–69) describing the problem, known bounds, and relevant references.
    51→- [x] Theorem docstring references source papers: [Er73], [Er75f], [Er80e], [KSS75].
    52→
    53→### `answer()` usage
    54→
    55→The original problem asks "Is it true that ℓ(N) ~ N^{1/2}?" — a yes/no question ("Is it true that ..."), so **Case 1** applies.
    56→
    57→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (lines 72–74).
    58→- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the question as originally asked: `Tendsto (fun N => (sidonSubsetNumber N : ℝ) / Real.sqrt N) atTop (nhds 1)`, which captures ℓ(N) ~ N^{1/2} (the ratio tends to 1).
    59→- [x] `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no" to the original question.
    60→- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)` (inside the RHS of `↔`), not as theorem parameters.
    61→
    62→## 7. Category Attribute
    63→
    64→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
    65→- [x] `research open` is a valid category value.
    66→- [x] Theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body (consistent with `research open`).
    67→
    68→## 8. AMS Attribute
    69→
    70→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 11`.
    71→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5 and 11).
    72→- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order: 5, 11.
    73→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    74→- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 11 (number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.
    75→
    76→## 9. Proof Bodies
    77→
    78→- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` format (lines 74–75, multi-line acceptable per checklist).
    79→- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    80→- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
    81→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    82→
    83→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    84→
    85→- N/A: No variants present. The problem is a single question.
    86→
    87→## 11. Code Quality
    88→
    89→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    90→- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `ℝ`, `ℕ`, `∀`, `∈`, `⊆`, `∧`, `∨`, `↔`, `→`.
    91→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    92→- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
    93→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    94→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    95→
    96→## 12. Build Verification
    97→
    98→- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review. No obvious issues; expected `sorry` warning only.
    99→- [ ] Custom linters not run during this review.
   100→
   101→---
   102→
   103→## Summary
   104→
   105→**Result: PASS** (all substantive items pass)
   106→
   107→The file is clean, well-structured, and fully conformant. The formalization is mathematically sound:
   108→
   109→- **Definitions** are well-layered: `IsSidonSet` defines the Sidon property, `maxSidonSubsetSize` captures the largest Sidon subset of a given set, and `sidonSubsetNumber` computes ℓ(N) as the infimum over all N-element sets — correctly formalizing the "worst-case" nature of the problem.
   110→- **Theorem statement** correctly uses Case 1 (`answer(sorry) ↔ P`) since the problem asks "Is it true that ...?", and the RHS captures asymptotic equivalence ℓ(N) ~ N^{1/2} via `Tendsto ... (nhds 1)`.
   111→- **AMS codes** are appropriate: 5 (combinatorics) and 11 (number theory) reflect the additive-combinatorial and number-theoretic nature of Sidon sets.
   112→- **Build verification** (§12): Not performed during this review; should be confirmed in CI.
   113→

file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/530.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 530`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/530`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines ℓ(N) as the maximum Sidon subset size guaranteed in any N-element real set, asks whether ℓ(N) ~ N^{1/2}).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: [Er73], [Er75f], [Er80e], [Ri69], [KSS75].

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos530 ... end Erdos530`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos530`).
- [x] `open Finset Filter` appears **before** the namespace block (line 41).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `IsSidonSet` (line 48) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 45–47).
- [x] `maxSidonSubsetSize` (line 53) has a LaTeX docstring (line 52).
- [x] `sidonSubsetNumber` (line 59) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 56–58).
- [x] `maxSidonSubsetSize` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sSup`, which involves classical choice).
- [x] `sidonSubsetNumber` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`, which involves classical choice).
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Prop uses `UpperCamelCase`: `IsSidonSet`.
- [x] Functions returning values use `lowerCamelCase`: `maxSidonSubsetSize`, `sidonSubsetNumber`.
- N/A: No `local notation` used (none needed).
- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional; "consider" item).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_530` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 62–69) describing the problem, known bounds, and relevant references.
- [x] Theorem docstring references source papers: [Er73], [Er75f], [Er80e], [KSS75].

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks "Is it true that ℓ(N) ~ N^{1/2}?" — a yes/no question ("Is it true that ..."), so **Case 1** applies.

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (lines 72–74).
- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the question as originally asked: `Tendsto (fun N => (sidonSubsetNumber N : ℝ) / Real.sqrt N) atTop (nhds 1)`, which captures ℓ(N) ~ N^{1/2} (the ratio tends to 1).
- [x] `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no" to the original question.
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)` (inside the RHS of `↔`), not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
- [x] `research open` is a valid category value.
- [x] Theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body (consistent with `research open`).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 11`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5 and 11).
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order: 5, 11.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 11 (number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` format (lines 74–75, multi-line acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. The problem is a single question.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `ℝ`, `ℕ`, `∀`, `∈`, `⊆`, `∧`, `∨`, `↔`, `→`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review. No obvious issues; expected `sorry` warning only.
- [ ] Custom linters not run during this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (all substantive items pass)

The file is clean, well-structured, and fully conformant. The formalization is mathematically sound:

- **Definitions** are well-layered: `IsSidonSet` defines the Sidon property, `maxSidonSubsetSize` captures the largest Sidon subset of a given set, and `sidonSubsetNumber` computes ℓ(N) as the infimum over all N-element sets — correctly formalizing the "worst-case" nature of the problem.
- **Theorem statement** correctly uses Case 1 (`answer(sorry) ↔ P`) since the problem asks "Is it true that ...?", and the RHS captures asymptotic equivalence ℓ(N) ~ N^{1/2} via `Tendsto ... (nhds 1)`.
- **AMS codes** are appropriate: 5 (combinatorics) and 11 (number theory) reflect the additive-combinatorial and number-theoretic nature of Sidon sets.
- **Build verification** (§12): Not performed during this review; should be confirmed in CI.

content.text: The review passed — **Result: PASS**. No changes needed.
